$comment: Pinned upstream binaries shipped inside this repository under bin/.
Each entry corresponds exactly to one file under bin/. The runtime
verifies the SHA256 at startup against this manifest before exec'ing.
To update a pinned binary:
1. Bump the version + url + sha256 fields below
2. Run `make sync-bins` (downloads + verifies)
3. Commit the changes (manifest + bin/) together
CI runs `make verify-bins` on every push to ensure manifest and bin/
stay in lockstep.
Privileged C++ daemon that owns route + DNS mutations. Source:
feivpn-apps/client/electron/{linux,macos}_proxy_controller. The Go
daemon (feivpn) talks to it over a local socket:
Linux  → unix:/var/run/feivpn_controller
Lifecycle is managed by feivpnctl as a separate systemd unit /
LaunchDaemon; feivpn.service Wants/After feivpn-router.service.
macOS shipping model mirrors the upstream Electron client: a single
Universal Binary (lipo arm64 + x86_64). Both darwin-arm64 and
darwin-amd64 keys point at bin/feivpn-router-darwin-universal — the
SHA must match because it's the same file on disk.
$changelog_v0_1_1: Linux: switch route hijack to policy-based routing (PBR).
Old behavior (v0.1.0): delete every default route in main table
and replace with `default via 10.0.85.2 dev feivpn-tun1`. This
blackholed every existing TCP session (incl. the operator's
own SSH) the moment configureRouting was acknowledged.
New behavior: leave main table untouched. Add `default via
10.0.85.2 dev feivpn-tun1` to private table 13579 + install
`ip rule add not fwmark 0x2333 lookup 13579 priority 29870`.
The fwmark matches DirectMarkValue in the Go daemon's
direct_dialer_linux.go so the daemon's outbound trojan socket
falls through to the physical NIC.
Linux: preserve inbound TCP via iptables CONNMARK (mangle table).
INPUT  -i <eth>  -j CONNMARK --set-mark 0x2333
OUTPUT -m connmark --mark 0x2333 -j MARK --set-mark 0x2333
Reply packets for SSH / locally-hosted services keep flowing
out the physical interface even after the policy rule flips on.
Linux: only the linux-amd64 binary is rebuilt in this release;
linux-arm64 / darwin-universal still ship the v0.1.0 codepath.
They will be rebuilt the next time the build pipeline runs on
a host that has docker linux/arm64 / a macOS toolchain.
